title: Body Fat Calculator by Skinfolds 2026 — Jackson-Pollock | Hacé Cuentas
description: Body fat calculator: estimate your body fat percentage using the Jackson-Pollock 3-site skinfold formula. Measure with calipers and get your category.
intro: **Body fat percentage** is a much more useful indicator than weight or BMI. This calculator uses the **Jackson-Pollock 3-site skinfold formula**, the standard in body assessment. You need a **skinfold caliper** to measure skinfolds in millimeters. It's the most accessible and reliable method outside of a lab.
keyTakeaway: **Jackson-Pollock formula**: 3 skinfolds → body density → % fat (Siri equation). Men: chest + abdomen + thigh. Women: triceps + suprailiac + thigh. Men at 12–18% and women at 20–28% are in the healthy range.
useCases: You're tracking your progress during a cutting phase and want to see fat loss.
Your trainer measures your skinfolds and you want to interpret the results.
You prefer a more accurate method than BMI to assess composition.
You're preparing for a fitness competition and need a specific %.
You're a PE professional assessing clients.
explanation:
## Jackson-Pollock 3-Site Formula

### Men (chest, abdomen, thigh)
```
Density = 1.10938 - 0.0008267 × Σ + 0.0000016 × Σ² - 0.0002574 × age
```

### Women (triceps, suprailiac, thigh)
```
Density = 1.0994921 - 0.0009929 × Σ + 0.0000023 × Σ² - 0.0001392 × age
```

Where Σ = sum of the 3 skinfolds in mm.

### Siri Equation
```
% Fat = (495 / density) - 450
```

## Body Fat Categories

| Category | Men | Women |
|---|---|---|
| Essential fat | 2–6% | 10–14% |
| Athlete | 6–14% | 14–21% |
| Fitness | 14–18% | 21–25% |
| Average | 18–25% | 25–32% |
| Overweight | >25% | >32% |

## How to Measure Skinfolds

1. **Use a caliper** (Slim Guide, Harpenden, or Lange).
2. Pinch the skinfold with **thumb and index**, 1 cm above the site.
3. Place the caliper **perpendicular** to the fold.
4. Read at **2–3 seconds** (before tissue compresses).
5. Take **3 measurements** and average. Always on the **right side**.

## Where to Measure Each Skinfold

- **Chest**: diagonal line between armpit and nipple, midpoint.
- **Abdomen**: vertical, 2 cm to the side of the navel.
- **Thigh**: vertical, midpoint between groin and kneecap.
- **Triceps**: vertical, back of the arm, midpoint.
- **Suprailiac**: diagonal, above the hip bone (iliac crest).

## Advantages Over BMI

BMI doesn't distinguish muscle from fat. A 185 lb / 5'9" man with 12% body fat has a BMI of 27.3 ('overweight') but excellent body composition. Skinfolds give you a real image.

faq.q: Do I need an expensive caliper?
faq.a: No. An economy caliper (Slim Guide, ~$5–10) is enough for personal tracking with ±3% error. Professionals use Harpenden (~$300) for higher precision.
Chest and abdomen skinfolds yes. Triceps and suprailiac are hard to measure alone. Ideally always have the **same person** measure you for consistency.

faq.q: How often should I measure skinfolds?
faq.a: Every **3–4 weeks**. Measuring more often doesn't make sense because changes are slow and measurement variability can confuse you.

faq.q: Is it more accurate than a bioimpedance scale?
faq.a: Generally **yes**, if measured by someone trained. Home bioimpedance scales have ±5–8% error, while skinfolds with a trained evaluator: ±3–4%.

faq.q: Does it work for people with a lot of overweight?
faq.a: Accuracy decreases when skinfolds are very thick (>40 mm). In those cases, **waist circumference** or **bioimpedance** may be more practical.

faq.q: Why are the skinfolds different for men and women?
faq.a: Because men and women accumulate fat in different zones. Men tend to accumulate in the **abdomen** (visceral fat), women in **hips, thighs, and triceps**.
For **men**: 10–20% is healthy, 6–14% athletic. For **women**: 18–28% healthy, 14–21% athletic. Below essential fat (2–6% M, 10–14% W) there's health risk.

howToSteps.name: Select your sex
howToSteps.text: Choose male or female (skinfolds and formulas differ).

howToSteps.name: Measure the 3 skinfolds
howToSteps.text: With a caliper, measure 3 skinfolds in mm (men: chest/abdomen/thigh; women: triceps/suprailiac/thigh).

howToSteps.name: Click Calculate
howToSteps.text: Press "Calculate" to get your body fat % and category.
